title: Equivariant Contact Floer Cohomology for Quotient Spaces
speaker: Eric Kilgore
affiliation: University of Southern California, Los Angeles
abstract: I will discuss some recent work establishing the orderability of contact manifolds which arise as a quotient of an aspherically fillable manifold by a finite group action which extends (non-freely) to the filling. This generalizes the well known case of lens spaces. The main tool is a geometrically equivariant version of contact Floer cohomology parametrized by a higher categorical refinement of the Eliashberg—Polterovich relation on the contact isotopy group, which is locally constant away from the discriminant. This is joint work with Dylan Cant and Jun Zhang.

proof_stages.label: Eliashberg-Polterovich orderability background
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls the nonnegative contact-isotopy relation on the universal cover of the contactomorphism group, its equivalence with absence of contractible positive loops, and its role as a rigidity condition in contact topology.

proof_stages.label: Nonfree finite-group fillings and main theorem
proof_stages.summary: A G-filling is defined as an aspherical convex-at-infinity symplectic filling carrying a Hamiltonian finite-group action whose quotient boundary is Y, and the main theorem states that a nonfree such action forces Y to be orderable.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to contact spectral invariants
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains that the actual result is the construction of contact spectral invariants, defined via discriminant points, spectra of contact isotopies, monotonicity, normalization, subadditivity, and Reeb-flow continuity, for every contact form on such a quotient.

proof_stages.label: Equivariant contact Floer cohomology functor
proof_stages.summary: The central Floer-theoretic tool is a functor from the category of nondiscriminant contact isotopies and nonnegative homotopies to K[x]-modules, satisfying finite generation, torsion mapping-cone, local-constancy, and PSS-type normalization properties.

proof_stages.label: Persistent canonical unit
proof_stages.summary: Using the small-Reeb-flow PSS normalization and fixed-point localization, the equivariant theory contains a distinguished free K[x]-tower generated by a unit, and the torsion mapping-cone axiom prevents that unit from dying under continuation maps.

proof_stages.label: Definition and spectrality of c_alpha
proof_stages.summary: For a contact isotopy phi, c_alpha(phi) is defined as the first Reeb time at which the persistent unit lies in the continuation image from HF_G(phi^{-1}R_alpha^s), and local constancy off the discriminant plus non-eternality of the unit shows that this value is spectral and yields the desired invariant.

proof_stages.label: Integer-valued conjugation-invariant measurement
proof_stages.summary: Under the stronger assumption that ordinary symplectic cohomology vanishes, the equivariant exact triangle makes x invertible, enabling an integer invariant mu defined by negative x-powers in the continuation image and satisfying monotonicity, normalization, unboundedness, and discriminant-detection properties.

proof_stages.label: Further monoidal and localized categorical structure
proof_stages.summary: The final part formulates open directions around quasimorphism properties, possible origins in noncontractible positive loops, product structures on a path-space or infinity-categorical refinement of the contact-isotopy category, and localization at nondiscriminant morphisms with rank-one cones at transverse discriminant crossings.

examples.name: Standard tight spheres as a fillability warning
examples.note: The standard contact sphere is used as a counterexample to the naive idea that good symplectic fillability alone implies orderability, motivating an additional hypothesis such as the nonfree equivariant filling condition.

examples.name: Stabilized cotangent quotients from almost-free finite-group actions
examples.note: For a finite group acting on a manifold with isolated fixed points, the induced action on a stabilized cotangent bundle gives orderable quotient contact manifolds, with the reflected-circle construction yielding an orderable contact structure on RP^3#RP^3.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker runs the quotient construction from an almost-free finite-group action through the cotangent bundle, stabilization, diagonal action, and resulting quotient

examples.name: Projective and lens spaces for the integer invariant
